message:
🌱 Add k0s: kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)? mission (#809)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k0s-1150-kube-vip-support-load-balancing-vip",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k0s: kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?",
+    "description": "kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k0s deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k0s version and configuration:\n```bash\nk0s version\nk0s status\n```\nThis feature requires a working k0s installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k0s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k0s configuration:\n```bash\nk0s version\nk0s kubectl get nodes -o wide\nk0s kubectl get pods -A\n```\nHi everyone,\n\nI just tried to install kube-vip (load balancing + VIP) on k0s control planes, but it wasn't possible since the control planes are isolated. I also tried to install kube-vip on k0s worker nodes, but it didn't work as well."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?",
+        "description": "you're right, I absolutely forgot that we propagate the custom port to the \"clients\" too automatically. Had to read our HA custom port test case for that. \ud83d\ude04 \n\nSo basically the only good option I see (apart from the client-side balancer thingy we're already working on) is to make the bind address\n```yaml\nfrontend kubeAPI\n    bind lb-ip:6443\n    default_backend kubeAPI_backend\nfrontend konnectivity\n    bind lb-ip:8132\n    default_backend konnectivity_backend\nfrontend controllerJoinAPI\n    bind lb-ip:9443\n    default_backend controllerJoinAPI_backend\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nk0s kubectl get nodes -o wide\nk0s kubectl get pods -A\nk0s kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"kube-vip support (load balancing + VIP)?\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "you're right, I absolutely forgot that we propagate the custom port to the \"clients\" too automatically. Had to read our HA custom port test case for that. \ud83d\ude04 \n\nSo basically the only good option I see (apart from the client-side balancer thingy we're already working on) is to make the bind address configurable so we can bind all controlplane components to some non VIP address on the host.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "frontend kubeAPI\n    bind lb-ip:6443\n    default_backend kubeAPI_backend\nfrontend konnectivity\n    bind lb-ip:8132\n    default_backend konnectivity_backend\nfrontend controllerJoinAPI\n    bind lb-ip:9443\n    default_backend controllerJoinAPI_backend",
+        "spec:\n  bindAddress: 1.2.3.4",
+        "spec:\n  bindAddress: 1.1.1.1\n  api:\n    externalAddress: 100.100.100.100"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "k0s",
+      "sandbox",
+      "orchestration",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "k0s"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/issues/1150",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s"
+    },
+    "reactions": 7,
+    "comments": 31,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "k0s",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running k0s cluster or a system where k0s can be installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:37:05.379Z",

+}
